q: A spectrometer is a scientific instrument used to separate and measure ___ components of a physical phenomenon.
The optical spectrometer consists of a light source, collimator, ___ element and a telescope.
Three of the most common optical spectrometers are spectrophotometers, spectrofluorometers and ___ ___.
In a spectrophotometer, a ___ element separates light into a spectrum.
The change in the absorption and emission of the light intensity with ___ allows for the identification of the materials.
The laser medium can be a gas, liquid, crystalline solid or ___ crystal.
The ___ ___ is supplied as an electric current, a flash lamp or another laser.
The optical cavity consists of two highly reflective ___.
A partial reflector allows some part of the light to come out as a ___ ___.
Atoms undergoing stimulated emission radiate photons of the same frequency in the same ___.
For stimulated emission to occur, a ___ ___ would be needed.
To sustain a laser action the lower level must be ___ while the upper level is populated.
A typical beam divergence is around a ___.
Lasers are used in the semiconductor industries for ___.
The refractive index of the core is $n_1 = 1.63$ and that of cladding is $n_2 = 1.5$ according to Example ___.
According to Snell's law, the ___ ___ for core to cladding interface is $i_c = 67^{\circ}$.
The optical spectrometer allows the user to rotate the telescope and determine the angle of dispersion for each ___ ___.
Because of refraction and absorption, the light beam ___ and dies off over a certain distance.
Laser range finders are used to determine the ___ to an object.
Lasers are used in shops and business establishments to collect information from the ___ ___ printed on the product.
The collimated beam from a specific light source is dispersed by a dispersion element such as ___ or grating.
A laser beam of wavelength $6328 \mathring{A}$ is emitted as shown in Example ___.
The cladding has a ___ refractive index.
The endoscope uses a bundle of ___ ___ to observe an image inside the body.
